stories.title: Thunder Complete Sweep of Lakers, Advance to Western Conference Finals

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The @OKCThunder are 8-0 in the 2026 playoffs.

SGA: 35 pts to close out the Lakers.
Holmgren: 18.6 ppg / 9.1 reb on 60% shooting for the series.

Thunder don't just beat you. They embarrass you. #ThunderUp

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Chet Holmgren averaged 18.6 pts, 9.1 reb and shot 60% in the Thunder's sweep of the Lakers.

He's not a sidekick anymore. He's a cornerstone. #ThunderUp #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Oklahoma City Thunder are a freight train that nobody can stop right now.

With a 115-110 win in Game 4 on Sunday night, OKC swept the Los Angeles Lakers out of the playoffs and advanced to the Western Conference Finals. The Thunder are now a perfect 8-0 in the 2026 postseason — and they aren't even breaking a sweat.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander delivered when it mattered most with 35 points in the clincher, but the real story of this series might be Chet Holmgren. The big man was an absolute force all series long, averaging 18.6 points and 9.1 rebounds per game while shooting an eye-popping 60.0% from the floor. In Game 1, he had 24 points, 12 rebounds, and 3 blocks. In Game 2, he posted 22 points with 4 steals. Game 4? Another 16 points and 9 boards.

For LeBron James, 41 years old and now staring at another early exit, the questions about retirement will only get louder this week.

OKC now awaits the winner of the Spurs-Timberwolves series, which San Antonio leads 3-2. Either way, the Thunder have already announced themselves as the team to beat in the West.

Who do you think has a better shot against OKC — San Antonio with Wemby, or Minnesota with Edwards?
facebook.image_caption: 8-0 in the 2026 playoffs. The Thunder swept the Lakers and they don't look like slowing down. SGA + Holmgren = the West's biggest problem. 🏆 #ThunderUp #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Thunder Are 8-0 and Just Getting Started After Sweeping the Lakers

stories.title: Wembanyama Dominates Game 5, Spurs Take 3-2 Series Lead Over Timberwolves

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Ejected in Game 4. Back in Game 5 with 27 pts, 17 reb, 5 ast, 3 blk.

Wemby scored 18 of those points IN THE FIRST QUARTER.

Spurs lead 3-2. One win from the Conference Finals. #GoSpursGo #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Victor Wembanyama had a message for the Minnesota Timberwolves after his Game 4 ejection, and he delivered it in the loudest possible way.

Wemby racked up 27 points, 17 rebounds, 5 assists, and 3 blocks in San Antonio's 126-97 rout of Minnesota on Tuesday night, giving the Spurs a 3-2 series lead heading into Game 6 on Friday.

The most jaw-dropping part? He scored 18 of those 27 points in the first quarter. When Minnesota called a timeout with 5:44 left in Q1, Wembanyama had already outscored the entire Timberwolves team 16-11 by himself.

That kind of performance makes history. He became the third-youngest player in NBA playoff history to record at least 25 points, 15 rebounds, and 5 assists in a single game, joining Magic Johnson and Luka Doncic on that list.

The Spurs dominated every aspect of this game, outscoring Minnesota 68-36 in points in the paint. Game 6 is Friday night at Target Center in Minneapolis. The Wolves need a win to stay alive.

Is Wemby already the most dominant force in the 2026 playoffs?
facebook.image_caption: Ejected in Game 4. Back in Game 5 with 27 pts, 17 reb, 5 ast, and 3 blk. Wemby is on another level. 🦁 #GoSpursGo #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Wemby Writes a New Chapter: 27/17/5 Carries Spurs Past Timberwolves in Game 5

stories.title: Knicks Sweep 76ers, Return to Eastern Conference Finals

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The @nyknicks swept the 76ers and tied the NBA playoff record with 25 made 3-pointers in Game 4.

Brunson: 29 ppg for the series.
McBride: 25 pts, 7 threes in the clincher.

New York is playing their best basketball. #NewYorkForever #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The New York Knicks are back in the Eastern Conference Finals — and they look like the team nobody in the East wants to face.

New York swept the Philadelphia 76ers with a dominant 144-114 win in Game 4 on Saturday, capping one of the most lopsided series in recent playoff memory. The Knicks outscored Philly by nearly 20 points per game across the entire sweep, which is the largest margin through two rounds since the playoffs went to 16 teams in 1984.

Jalen Brunson was spectacular all series, averaging 29.0 points and 6.0 assists per game. But the real breakout moment came from Deuce McBride in the clincher — the reserve guard went off for 25 points and connected on 7 three-pointers, tying the NBA postseason record for made 3s in a single game. He was 4-for-4 from deep in the first quarter alone.

The Knicks now wait to see if they'll face the Cleveland Cavaliers or the Detroit Pistons in the Conference Finals. Game 5 of that series tips off tonight in Detroit.

Madison Square Garden is going to be electric. Who do you think makes it out of the Cavs-Pistons series to face New York?
facebook.image_caption: Sweep. Done. Back to the ECF. Brunson averaging 29 ppg, McBride tying the NBA playoff record for 3-pointers in a game. The Knicks are rolling. 🗽 #NewYorkForever #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: New York's Machine: Knicks Sweep 76ers to Return to the Eastern Conference Finals

stories.title: Cavaliers-Pistons Game 5 Tonight — Mitchell vs. Cunningham in Detroit

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
GAME NIGHT: Cavaliers vs. Pistons, Game 5.

Mitchell: 43 pts in Game 4.
Cade: 31.8 pts / 8.2 ast at home this postseason.

Series tied 2-2. Little Caesars Arena. 8 PM ET on ESPN. Who wins? #DetroitBasketball #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Everything changes tonight in Detroit.

Game 5 of the Cavaliers-Pistons series tips off at 8:00 PM ET on ESPN from Little Caesars Arena, with the series all knotted up at 2-2. After the Pistons took the first two games, Cleveland stormed back with two straight wins — including Donovan Mitchell's 43-point masterpiece in Game 4.

Now the question is: can Detroit find an answer?

Cade Cunningham has been a monster in this series, especially at home, averaging 31.8 points and 8.2 assists across his six home games this postseason. In a building that has been electric all playoffs, Cade and the Pistons will need every bit of that home energy to push back against a Cavs team riding a surge of momentum.

The winner of this series faces the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference Finals.

Two teams that did NOT see this series going 5+ games. Two stars ready to take over. Who takes it tonight — Mitchell or Cade?
facebook.image_caption: Game 5 tonight. Detroit. 8 PM ET on ESPN. Mitchell had 43 in Game 4. Cade is nearly unstoppable at home. This one has classic written all over it. 🔥 #DetroitBasketball #LetEmKnow #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Cade vs. Mitchell, Game 5: Everything on the Line Tonight in Detroit

stories.title: Spurs-Timberwolves Game 6 Preview — Can Minnesota Force a Game 7?

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Wolves are on the brink. Spurs lead 3-2.

Edwards had 36/16 in Game 4. In Game 5, Wemby held Minnesota to 97 points and was in a different stratosphere.

Game 6: Friday in Minneapolis. Season on the line. #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Timberwolves have to go to the Target Center on Friday and win — or their season is over.

San Antonio leads the series 3-2 after Victor Wembanyama's monster performance in Game 5, where Minnesota lost 126-97 in one of the most lopsided games of the playoffs. Anthony Edwards had been the Wolves' best hope, going for 36 points and 16 rebounds in Game 4, but in Game 5 he was largely neutralized as Wemby completely took over.

Game 6 goes Friday, May 15, back in Minneapolis. Minnesota is 2-1 at home in this series, so there's reason for hope. But the way Wemby is playing, the Spurs have momentum and a generational talent who seems locked in.

If San Antonio closes it out, they'll meet the unbeaten Thunder in the Western Conference Finals — an OKC team that hasn't lost a single game in the 2026 playoffs.

Can Edwards and the Wolves flip the script at home and force a Game 7?
facebook.image_caption: Wolves' season on the line. Spurs lead 3-2. Game 6 Friday in Minneapolis. Can Edwards and Minnesota force a Game 7? #NBAPlayoffs

article.headline: Can Minnesota Force a Game 7? Wolves Face Elimination Friday vs. the Spurs
